titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Estate of Sinclair Lewis Records
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff, Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:26

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English, with a small amount of material in German.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, legal papers, financial papers, and personal papers relating to Sinclair Lewis, his wives Grace Hegger and Dorothy Thompson, and the Lewis family. Included are divorce decrees, custody and alimony agreements, wills, tax and real estate records, powers of attorney, and contracts pertaining to Lewis's novels Ann Vickers , Dodsworth , and Elmer Gantry . The collection also contains a few objects, such as metal keys and a visor, and a certificate of vaccinations against typhoid fever and smallpox for Sinclair, Grace, and Wells Lewis.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2011.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten series: I. Agreements, 1914-1941. II. Ann Vickers, circa 1930-1933. III. Correspondence, 1916-1948. IV. Divorce, 1928-1946. V. Financial Papers, 1880-1942. VI. Personal Papers, 1921-1929. VII. Powers of Attorney, 1928-1937. VIII. Real Estate, 1841-1949. IX. Taxes, 1917-1932. X. Wills, 1924-1941.

bioghist.head: Sinclair Lewis (1885-1951)
bioghist.p: Sinclair Lewis is best known as a novelist and social critic, who also wrote plays, poetry, and short fiction. Lewis was awarded the Pulitzer Prize in 1926 and, in 1930, he was the first American author to win the Nobel Prize for Literature.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Estate of Sinclair Lewis Records. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, legal papers, financial papers, and personal papers relating to Sinclair Lewis, his wives Grace Hegger and Dorothy Thompson, and the Lewis family. Included are divorce decrees, custody and alimony agreements, wills, tax and real estate records, powers of attorney, and contracts pertaining to Lewis's novels Ann Vickers , Dodsworth , and Elmer Gantry . The collection also contains a few objects, such as metal keys and a visor, and a certificate of vaccinations against typhoid fever and smallpox for Sinclair, Grace, and Wells Lewis.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Estate of Sinclair Lewis Records is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains publishing contracts, correspondence, and agreements to produce dramatizations of works by Sinclair Lewis, including Dodsworth and Elmer Gantry . Also included are trust, alimony, and custody agreements with Grace Hegger Lewis and Dorothy Thompson Lewis.

did.unittitle: Sinclair Lewis and Grace Hegger Lewis
Sinclair Lewis and Dorothy Thompson Lewis
Sinclair Lewis and Paul H. De Kruif
Sinclair Lewis and John T. Wildberg
Sinclair Lewis, Gilbert Mille, and Sidney Howard "Dodsworth"
Sinclair Lewis, Lloyd Lewis, and Harry Hammond, Inc.
Sinclair Lewis, Pat Kearney Elmer Gantry

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains papers related to the development and publication of the novel Ann Vickers , including contracts, correspondence, cancelled checks, and an early sketch of the story and biography of the main character by Sinclair Lewis.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains papers related to Sinclair Lewis's literary endeavors, including correspondence with his agent Ann Watkins and publishing and motion picture executives; legal correspondence and documents concerning Thorvale Farm, Lewis's property in Williamstown, Massachusetts; and trust indentures with his former wife Grace Hegger Lewis. Also included is a 1921 certificate of inoculation of Lewis family members against typhoid and smallpox, signed by a physician.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes legal papers related to Sinclair Lewis's divorces from Grace Hegger Lewis and Dorothy Thompson Lewis, as well as a custody agreement for Michael Lewis.

did.unittitle: Divorce decree Grace Hegger Lewis and Sinclair Lewis
Release of alimony Grace Hegger Lewis
General release Grace Hegger Lewis and Sinclair Lewis
Deed of trust Sinclair Lewis and Grace Lewis
Instrument altering and modifying indenture...Sinclair Lewis and Grace Hegger Lewis
Modification and alteration of trust indenture Sinclair Lewis and Grace Hegger Lewis
Divorce decree Dorothy Thompson Lewis and Sinclair Lewis

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes bills, receipts, bank records, and checkbooks, as well as several royalty statements from Harcourt Brace and Company to Sinclair Lewis.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes several metal keys, a green eyeshade, a British license to drive issued to Sinclair Lewis, and a marriage certificate of Lewis and Dorothy Thompson.

did.unittitle: Marriage license: Sinclair Lewis and Dorothy Thompson

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of powers of attorney Sinclair Lewis granted to his manager Melville H. Cane, publisher Alfred Harcourt, and lawyer Bernard M. L. Lewis.

did.unittitle: Sinclair Lewis to Melville H. Cane and Bernard M. L. Ernest
Sinclair Lewis to Melville H. Cane

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains deeds, correspondence, documentation of property value, and other records pertaining to Thorvale Farm.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of income tax returns of Sinclair Lewis and related documents and notes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of the wills of Sinclair Lewis, Grace Hegger Lewis, and Dorothy Thompson Lewis.
